summary: Hotz laments that modern life has lost visceral, meaningful experiences—the adrenaline of survival, the stakes of real challenges. As AI advances, even 'Type 2 experiences' requiring serious effort will disappear, leaving humanity disengaged and purposeless. He sees his mission at comma.ai and tinygrad as giving individuals deep, structural control over technology—not surface-level permissions that can be revoked. The coming AI revolution will outcompete 'spiritual bottoms' (passive consumers), bifurcating humanity into those who control technology and those who wirehead themselves out of relevance. His goal is maximizing the number of humans who retain real control, which he believes is his best chance of survival.
key_points: Modern survival has become 'technocratic plod'—all remaining visceral experiences are fake (games, sports, drugs) or stupid (reckless driving)
ASI will eliminate Type 2 experiences (achievements requiring serious effort), leaving only effortless consumption or impossible goals
True technological accessibility means giving people deep, structural control—not permissions that can be revoked
AI is the 'ultimate spiritual bottom' and will outcompete passive human consumers, causing humanity to bifurcate
Technology should be built inseparable from your narrative, because on a long enough timeline it ends up in your enemy's hands
money_quotes: Survival has become a technocratic plod, making the right boring and careful decisions.
Even when humanity goes to Mars, we will be going as cargo.
Knowing the future doesn't help you change it.
I am continually shocked at how little people understand about anything, they don't even understand that they don't understand.
The ghost gunner is the real second amendment.
AI is the ultimate spiritual bottom.
Humanity bifurcates. Humans will retain control for the foreseeable future, the only question is, how many humans?
Build technology and structures that are inseparable from the narrative you want, as opposed to ones you think you can wield for good.
key_insight: As AI eliminates meaningful human challenges, the only defense is building technology that structurally empowers individuals—not products that can be co-opted or permissions that can be revoked.
